name: Edge: customer-tier extreme — only STANDARD customers + delay slightly exceeds cover
rubric_notes: Delay (120h) just barely exceeds cover (100h) → Rule 2 fires immediate_switch. But the only customers at risk are STANDARD-tier, AND their deadlines (110h, 130h) are within reach if stockout is brief (~20h gap between cover depletion and primary recovery). A 20-hour stockout with only STANDARD-tier exposure is a defensibly tolerable outcome — wait_and_monitor with accepted brief stockout is rational. acceptable_actions tolerates both. This scenario tests whether the system rightly distinguishes 'rule fires' from 'rule's underlying economics make sense.' Severity MEDIUM (delay exceeds but no high-tier pressure). Confidence MEDIUM because the switch-vs-tolerate-brief-stockout is a genuine tradeoff for low-stakes customers.
